1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 077 — **three repair / re-attempt lanes** alongside the iter-077 refactor-subagent scaffold of `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` already in place from this plan pass).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill — re-opened iter-076 per user directive 2026-05-12) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073 by reduction).
18	
19	This plan pass has already:
20	- Verified iter-076's two repair lanes both compile cleanly (Differentials 7 sorries, BasicOpenCech 7 sorries — both error-free).
21	- Dispatched the refactor subagent and scaffolded `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` (Phase C step C0; 3 new sorries: `tensorObj`, `instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`).
22	- **Detected a previously-mis-reported critical issue**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` does **NOT** compile (6 errors); inherited from archon[073/prover] and silently propagated through iter-074/075/076 plans. Cascade: `AbelJacobi.lean` fails because its upstream `.olean` doesn't exist.
23	
24	After verifying with `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass, the actual project state is:
25	
26	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-077 plan |
27	|---|---|---|---|---|
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 7 | yes | A | **Lane 2: re-attempt iter-076 rollbacks** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 7 | yes | B | **Lane 1: re-attempt iter-076 rollbacks** |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | **NO** (6 errors) | D | **Lane 3: REPAIR compilation** |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | **NO** (cascade) | E | resolves automatically once Lane 3 lands |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 3 | yes | C0 | off-limits (just scaffolded; bodies for iter-078+) |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
35	| **Total syntactic** | **19** | — | | |
36	
37	The previously-PROGRESS-claimed "Jacobian.lean compiles" was **incorrect** — verified directly via `lake env lean` and `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass. The errors have been latent since the iter-073 archon prover commit (`6f20a1a2 archon[073/prover]`); subsequent plan passes (iter-074, iter-075, iter-076) accepted the file's status from the cached PROGRESS.md without independent verification. This is the kind of stale-claim issue the plan agent is specifically supposed to detect.
38	
39	## Current Objectives
40	
41	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
42	
43	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — RE-ATTEMPT iter-076 rollbacks
44	
45	**File status.** 7 syntactic sorries; **file compiles cleanly** (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, L207 `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, L232 `cotangentExactSeqBeta`, L266 `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (absorbs former L604 `h_exact` + L617 `h_epi`), L550 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L567 `cotangent_at_section`, L703 `serre_duality_genus`.
46	
47	**Goal.** Close `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L207) and/or `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L232) — and ideally also `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L266) by re-enabling the preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block. The L122 / L550 / L567 / L703 sorries are out of scope for this lane.
48	
49	**Re-attempt recipe** (each error individually addressed):
50	
51	- **`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L207).** Five specific fixes from the iter-076 error inventory:
52	  1. **L244** (additivity of the `d_target.d` AddMonoidHom). Replace `(by intro a b; simp)` with `by intro a b; rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_add, D_X.d_add]`.
53	  2. **L248** (`d_mul`). Insert explicit `show D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a * b)) = a • D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b) + b • D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom a)` *before* the `rw`. Then `rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]; rfl`.
54	  3. **L256** (`d_map`). Type-annotate the `h` binder in `congr_arg`: `have := congr_arg (fun h : _ ⟶ _ => (ConcreteCategory.hom h) x) (f.c.naturality f')` (or pre-bind `let h_eq := f.c.naturality f'`).
55	  4. **L258** — same shape as L248: insert explicit `show` to commit the `(f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x)` form.
56	  5. **L266** (`d_app`'s `hcomm` step). The `rfl` claim for `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward).map φ_fg'` fails — this is the adjunction-coherence identity, **not definitional**. Derive via `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` + `Equiv.symm_apply_eq`. *If non-trivial in-iteration*: roll back just the `d_app` block to `sorry` (preserve the rest of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`).
57	
58	- **`cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L232).** Recipe:
59	  - Prepend `set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in` to the `noncomputable def`.
60	  - Remove the redundant `exact h4` at the former L332 (the prior `simp only` / `erw [h6]` chain already closes the goal).
61	  - Or inline `η` rather than `set`-ing it, to reduce elaboration cost.
62	
63	- **`cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L266).** Once `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`/`Beta` compile, re-enable the preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block (Steps 1–7 of `h_zero` with `hα_fac`/`hβ_fac`/`hd_app`/`hcoh`). The `unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha; unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta` invocations inside that block depend on the upstream defs compiling — once they do, the block should close `_structure.h_zero` (and the formerly-separate `h_exact`/`h_epi` route reduces to `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` + `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` + `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`).
64	
65	**Hard constraints**:
66	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
67	- **No new axioms.**
68	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies (user policy). `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop).
69	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 7; **target ≤ 5** (close at least one of alpha/beta/structure); hard cap **7** (no regression).
70	
71	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
72	
73	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — RE-ATTEMPT iter-076 rollbacks
74	
75	**File status.** 7 syntactic sorries; **file compiles cleanly** (0 errors). Sorries: L495, L819, L847 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), L1077 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, L1106 `f_R.map_smul'`, L1116 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1145 `h_loc_exact`.
76	
77	**Goal.** Close one or more of `f_R.map_smul'` (L1106), `g_R.map_smul'` (L1116), `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1077), and downstream `h_loc_exact` (L1145). The L495/L819/L847 dead-ends are out of scope.
78	
79	**Re-attempt recipe**. The iter-076 root-cause analysis identified **instance literality** as the hard blocker: `h_mod_X_i` was constructed via `convert h` (and `convert h using N`), producing a `Module R scK₀.X_i` instance that differs from the canonical `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` by residual eta-congruence. As a result, in `f_R.map_smul'`/`g_R.map_smul'` the `congr 1` step for `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` cannot close by `rfl`.
80	
81	Additionally, for X₃: `(ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n` does **NOT** reduce to `n + 1` by `rfl` (opaque `Classical.choose`); use `CochainComplex.next` (Mathlib lemma: `(ComplexShape.up α).next i = i + 1`). The iter-076 working pattern for `h_mod_X₃`:
82	```lean
83	have h_mod_X₃ : Module R scK₀.X₃ := by
84	  have h_eq : scK₀.X₃ = K₀.X (n + 1) := by
85	    show K₀.X ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n) = K₀.X (n + 1)
86	    rw [CochainComplex.next]
87	  rw [h_eq]
88	  dsimp [K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]
89	  letI := h_mod_pi₃
90	  have h : Module R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃) := e₃.toAddEquiv.module R
91	  convert h
92	```
93	This pattern fixes the L952 error, but the trailing `convert h` still leaves the instance non-literal. To make `f_R.map_smul'`/`g_R.map_smul'` close by `rfl`, **bypass `convert`** entirely: use explicit `Eq.mpr` casting on a manual type-level equality `↑scK₀.X_i = ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` so the instance is *literally* `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R`.
94	
95	Steps (in order):
96	1. **Rebuild `h_mod_X_i`** via explicit `Eq.mpr`/`cast` instead of `convert h`. For X₃, prepend a `CochainComplex.next` rewrite (as shown). The aim: `(Module R scK₀.X_i).toSMul` equals `e_i.toEquiv.smul R` *literally*.
97	2. **With literal `h_mod_X_i` in hand**, the iter-072 `calc` chains in `f_R.map_smul'` / `g_R.map_smul'` work as written (the `congr 1` for `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` will close by `rfl` because the smul comes from `e_i.toEquiv.smul R`).
98	3. **Then `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1077)**: attack via the documented S1–S8 recipe (decomposition of the 5-layer functor stack into per-summand R-linearity). The preserved comment block at L1077 documents the recipe.
99	4. **`h_loc_exact` (L1145)**: downstream — closes by `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (or `exact_of_localized_span`) once the per-summand chain compiles.
100	
101	**Note: `h_diff_pi_smul_g`** declaration was DROPPED iter-076 (un-typeable). **DO NOT re-add it.** If `g_R.map_smul'` needs an analogue of `h_diff_pi_smul_f` for the g-side, either reformulate the statement to avoid the `e₃`-codomain mismatch (use a `change`/`convert` inside the proof body) or split `g_R.map_smul'` so it doesn't need a free-standing `h_diff_pi_smul_g`.
102	
103	**Hard constraints**:
104	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
105	- **No new axioms.**
106	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.**
107	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 7; **target ≤ 5**; hard cap **7**.
108	
109	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Čech acyclicity*; also `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`.
110	
111	### 3. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — REPAIR compilation (NEW PRIORITY)
112	
113	**File status.** 1 syntactic sorry (L177 `nonempty_jacobianWitness` — protected by `nonempty_jacobianWitness`'s deferral to Phase C step C3); **file does NOT compile** — 6 errors, latent since archon[073/prover]:
114	
115	1. **L59 col 6**: `unexpected token 'ι'; expected '_' or identifier` inside `def IsAlbanese ... := ∃ (ι : C ⟶ J), ...`. Likely a parser-recovery from an upstream elaboration issue in the L57 `def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop`. Hypothesis: the outer `variable {k : Type u} [Field k]` is not auto-binding through the `namespace IsAlbanese` boundary, leaving `k` unresolved.
116	2. **L67 col 43** and **L71 col 43**: `typeclass instance problem is stuck — Field ?m.38`. Both occur in `noncomputable def ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J` (and the analogous `lemma comp_ofCurve`). The implicit `{C P J}` doesn't declare `k`, and the outer `variable {k : Type u} [Field k]` is not propagating. Likely fix: explicitly add `{k : Type u} [Field k]` to the implicit binders of `ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`, OR move the `variable` declaration INTO `namespace IsAlbanese`.
117	3. **L90 col 3**: `Invalid match expression: The type of pattern variable 'g' contains metavariables: J₁ ⟶ ?m.105`. 1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing

95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors

123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks

8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.
101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/
117	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen
118	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
119	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
121	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
122	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
123	          IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s) ∧
124	          HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)) :
125	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F where
126	  exists_cover {U} hU := by
127	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hacy, hcomp⟩ := h hU
128	    exact ⟨s, basicOpenCover s, basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top hU s hs, hacy, hcomp⟩
129	
130	/-- Iter-055: curve specialisation of `hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` at
131	`F := toModuleKSheaf C`.
132	
133	Mirrors the iter-039/042/043/048/049/050/051/052/053 `_curve` pattern: a thin
134	dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the
135	structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-055 producer
136	is chained through. Used by the iter-057+ packaging step to instantiate
137	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` once iter-056+ supplies the
138	per-affine basic-open Čech-acyclic + comparison-iso evidence. -/
139	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen_curve
140	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
141	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
142	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
143	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
144	          IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s) ∧
145	          HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)) :
146	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) :=
147	  hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen h
148	
149	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection identification.
150	
151	Given a Set `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` and indices `i j : s`, the intersection of the
152	two basic-open cover members `basicOpenCover s i` and `basicOpenCover s j`
153	equals the basic open of the product `i.1 * j.1`. Direct iteration of Mathlib's
154	`Scheme.basicOpen_mul`.
155	
156	This is the foundational identification for iter-057+ Čech complex evaluation:
157	the Čech cochain at degree 1 indexes pairs `(i, j) : s × s` and assigns the

401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology

412	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
413	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
414	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
415	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
416	schemes in our setting.
417	
418	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
419	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
420	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
421	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
422	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
423	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
424	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
425	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
426	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
428	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
429	
430	The structural skeleton is:
431	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
432	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
433	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
434	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
435	
436	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
437	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
438	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
439	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
440	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
441	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
442	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
443	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
444	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
445	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
446	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
447	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
448	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
449	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
450	
451	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
452	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
453	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
454	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
455	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
456	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
457	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
458	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
459	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
460	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
461	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
462	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
463	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
464	    -- The goal is now
465	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
466	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
467	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
468	    --
469	    -- ===========================================================================
470	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
471	    -- ===========================================================================
472	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
473	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
474	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
475	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
476	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
477	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
478	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
479	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
480	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
481	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
482	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
483	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
484	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
485	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
486	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
487	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
488	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
489	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
490	      intro f
491	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
492	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
493	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
494	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
495	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
496	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
497	    -- ===========================================================================
498	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
499	    -- ===========================================================================
500	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
501	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
502	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
503	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
504	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
505	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
506	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
507	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
508	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
509	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
510	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
511	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:
512	    -- `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]` when `f` is also among the generators).
513	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
514	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
515	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
516	    --
517	    -- ===========================================================================
518	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
519	    -- ===========================================================================
520	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at
521	    -- degree `n` of the slice cover Čech complex, which by substep (b) is
522	    -- the localisation of `K.sc n` — fits the hypothesis of
523	    -- `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...`
524	    -- (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean` L173) viewing
525	    -- the cochain factors as `Γ(C.left, U)`-modules through their natural
526	    -- module structure. The conclusion is `Function.Exact ⇑(K.sc n).f ⇑(K.sc n).g`,
527	    -- which converts back to `K.ExactAt n` via
528	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` and
529	    -- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff`.
530	    --
531	    -- This combined assembly remains as a single transient sorry: it
532	    -- requires constructing the cochain-level localisation iso (substep (b)
533	    -- ≈ a `noncomputable def` for the localised Čech short complex plus
534	    -- its identification iso) and routing the ring-base mismatch
535	    -- (`(K.sc n).f.hom` is `k`-linear; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes
536	    -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear maps — they coincide as set-functions, but the
537	    -- type-level reconciliation is non-trivial).
538	    --
539	    -- For iter-061, substep (a)'s claim is concretely stated above and
540	    -- substep (b)+(c)'s assembly remains as the second labelled sorry below.
541	    -- We perform the mechanical conversion `K.ExactAt n ↔ Function.Exact ⇑f ⇑g`
542	    -- before the sorry, so the remaining content is precisely the
543	    -- "transport per-f exactness through the localisation iso then apply
544	    -- the local-to-global principle" assembly step.
545	    rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
546	        ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact]
547	    -- Goal:
548	    --   `Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).f)
549	    --                   ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).g)`
550	    -- where `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
551	    -- ===========================================================================
552	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (a): convert `h_a` into `Function.Exact` form.
553	    -- ===========================================================================
554	    -- The same `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` +
555	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` chain that
556	    -- converts our goal also converts each per-`f` slice-cover `ExactAt n`
557	    -- hypothesis into a per-`f` `Function.Exact` statement at the
558	    -- function-level. This is closed under `← rw` (no new sorry).
559	    have h_a_fun : ∀ (f : s),
560	        Function.Exact
561	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
562	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
563	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
564	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).f)
565	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
566	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
567	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
568	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).g) := by
569	      intro f
570	      rw [← ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
571	          ← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
572	      exact h_a f
573	    -- ===========================================================================
574	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (b): outline the local-to-global route.
575	    -- ===========================================================================
576	    -- The closure of the remaining goal proceeds via Mathlib's local-to-global
577	    -- principle for exactness `exact_of_isLocalized_span` applied to the
578	    -- ring `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, the spanning subset `s ⊆ R`, and the
579	    -- `R`-linear maps `(K.sc n).f.hom` and `(K.sc n).g.hom` (treated as
580	    -- `R`-linear via the `Module R ((K.sc n).X i)`-structure obtained from
581	    -- the cochain factor's natural `Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`-module structure
582	    -- precomposed with the algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`).
583	    -- The required input is, for each `f ∈ s`, a `Function.Exact`
584	    -- statement on the localised cochain factors at `f`. By the
585	    -- cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
586	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
587	    -- commutation), the localised `(K.sc n).X i [1/f]` identifies with the
588	    -- slice-cover Čech `.X i` at the same degree, and consequently the
589	    -- localised differential identifies with the slice-cover differential.
590	    -- This identification + `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports
591	    -- `h_a_fun f` (which is the slice-cover Čech `Function.Exact` at
592	    -- degree `n`) to `Function.Exact` of the localised differential.
593	    -- The infinite-set subtlety (`s` is `Set`, not `Finset`) is sidestepped
594	    -- because `Ideal.span s = ⊤` already implies a *finite* subset spans
595	    -- the unit ideal — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` only consumes the per-`r`
596	    -- localised exactness data and the spanning hypothesis, not finiteness
597	    -- of `s` directly.
598	    -- ===========================================================================
599	    -- Substep (b1) — install the `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each
600	    -- cochain factor `(K.sc n).X i`.
601	    -- ===========================================================================
602	    -- The cochain factor `K.X i = ∏_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))`
603	    -- carries a `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure pointwise, since each
604	    -- `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the
605	    -- restriction map (iter-058's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le` supplies
606	    -- the inclusion `V ≤ U`, iter-006's `algebraSection` supplies the
607	    -- algebra structure on each factor).
608	    --
609	    -- ===========================================================================
610	    -- Substep (b2) — exhibit `slice_K.X i` as `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
611	    -- of `(K.sc n).X i` for each `f ∈ s`.
612	    -- ===========================================================================
613	    -- The cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
614	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
615	    -- commutation) gives
616	    --   `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰_slice(x k))`
617	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k) ⊓ D(f))`
618	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))[1/f]`        (iter-058 + iter-059)
619	    --                = `(∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k)))[1/f]`     (product-loc commutation)
620	    --                = `(K.X i)[1/f]`.
621	    -- The pointwise localisation map (component-wise restriction map
622	    -- `Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, V')` for `V' = V ⊓ D(f) ≤ V`) is the canonical
623	    -- localisation map and an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance.
624	    --
625	    -- ===========================================================================
626	    -- Substep (c) — apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ... h_a_fun`.
627	    -- ===========================================================================
628	    -- With the data from (b1) and (b2), `exact_of_isLocalized_span` packages
629	    -- the per-`f` exactness `h_a_fun f` into the global `Function.Exact`
630	    -- conclusion. The remaining work is purely the unification of the per-`f`
631	    -- input shape with the conclusion of `IsLocalizedModule.map ... F`
632	    -- via the iso from (b2) and `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`.
633	    -- ===========================================================================
634	    -- Iter-063 progress: explicit finite-subspanning extraction + obstruction
635	    -- diagnosis.
636	    -- ===========================================================================

224	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
225	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
226	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) =
227	      C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U))) := by
228	  classical
229	  induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
230	  | empty => exact absurd h Finset.not_nonempty_empty
231	  | cons a t ha ih =>
232	    rcases t.eq_empty_or_nonempty with rfl | hne
233	    · simp [basicOpenCover]
234	    · rw [Finset.inf'_cons hne, Finset.prod_cons, ih hne]
235	      change C.left.basicOpen (a.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) ⊓ _ = _
236	      rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
237	
238	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself affine.
239	
240	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, `(t : Finset s)`, and
241	`(h : t.Nonempty)`, the n-ary intersection `t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is
242	itself affine. Term-mode body chains
243	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting the n-ary intersection
244	as a basic open of the finite product) with Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
245	(membership preservation under basic-open formation).
246	
247	Generalises iter-056's `basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen` (pairwise affine
248	membership) to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing. Used by iter-058+ Čech
249	evaluation: each finite intersection of basic opens of an affine is itself
250	affine, which is the inductive step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument
251	(Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul
252	resolution).
253	
254	**Body uses `▸` (Eq.mpr), NOT `rw`**: `rw` on the equation produced by Theorem
255	(1) fails because of motive-occurrence issues with the implicit `Subtype.val`
256	coercions on elements of `Finset s`; term-mode `▸` rewrite resolves these
257	directly without invoking the rewriter's pattern-matching. Plan-agent
258	`lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirmed the term-mode form compiles
259	kernel-only while the tactic-mode `rw` form fails with "Did not find an
260	occurrence of the pattern" error. -/
261	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen
262	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
263	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
264	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
265	    IsAffineOpen (t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
266	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸ hU.basicOpen _
267	
268	/-- Iter-058: n-ary basic-open intersection is contained in `U`.
269	
270	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
271	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` is contained in `U`. Term-mode
272	body chains iter-057's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting
273	the n-ary intersection as a single basic open of the finite product) with
274	Mathlib's `Scheme.basicOpen_le` (`X.basicOpen f ≤ U` for any `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`)
275	via Lean's `▸` (Eq.mpr) rewrite.
276	
277	**No `IsAffineOpen` hypothesis required**: `basicOpen_le` holds for any open `U`
278	(the basic open of `f ∈ Γ(X, U)` is always a sub-open of `U`); compare iter-057's
279	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` which does need `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`.
280	
281	This is the inclusion morphism iter-059+ Čech-evaluation arguments need when
282	applying Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`:
283	```
284	(hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
285	  V = X.basicOpen f → IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)
286	```
287	The morphism `i : V ⟶ U` argument (which is `homOfLE` of `V ≤ U`) is needed to
288	install the `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` instance for the conclusion type, since
289	Mathlib's `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires for the
290	syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, not for `V = t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)`.
291	Iter-058's inclusion lemma supplies this `V ≤ U` proof so iter-059+ can call
292	`hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen f (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le ...))
293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land
294	`IsLocalization.Away (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s))`
295	directly. -/
296	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le
297	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
298	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
299	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
300	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) ≤ U :=
301	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸
302	    C.left.basicOpen_le _
303	
304	/-- Iter-059: n-ary basic-open intersection is a localization.
305	
306	Given an affine open `U`, a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, and a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`,
307	the structure sheaf evaluated on the n-ary intersection of basic-open cover members
308	`t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is identified — as a localization away from the product
309	`∏ i ∈ t, i.1` of generators — with `Γ(C.left, U)`. The algebra structure on the conclusion
310	type is given explicitly by `(C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op).hom.toAlgebra`, threaded
311	through the conclusion `@IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X, V) _ algebra` form (matching
312	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` signature exactly).
313	
314	**Explicit `@`-algebra binding is essential.** Mathlib's default
315	`algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean` L724)
316	fires only for the syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, *not* for arbitrary `V`
317	definitionally equal to `X.basicOpen f`. The iter-058 plan-agent probe-confirmed that
318	stating this theorem with *inferred* algebra fails with
319	`failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra Γ(C.left, U) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h …)`.
320	Threading the algebra structure as part of the conclusion type — matching Mathlib's
321	signature exactly — sidesteps the synthesis attempt.
322	
323	This is the **fourth and final** thin foundational scaffolding helper on the affine
324	basic-open identification side. The iter-060+ basic-open Koszul acyclicity argument
325	(per the iter-059 analogy report at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, Branch A
326	substep 2) chains iter-057's equality + iter-058's inclusion + iter-059's localization
327	+ `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean`)
328	to identify each Čech-cochain factor `F(⋂_k 𝒰(x k))` with the cover-of-`D(f)` Čech complex
329	on localised modules. -/
330	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization
331	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
332	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
333	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
334	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)))
335	      Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) _
336	      ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE
337	        (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h)).op).hom.toAlgebra) :=
338	  hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen _
339	    (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h))
340	    (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod (C := C) (U := U) s t h)
341	
342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec
392	
393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.

s : Set ↑Γ(C.left, U)

n : ℕ

h_a_fun :

K₀ : CochainComplex (ModuleCat k) ℕ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover ↑s₀)

h_a₀ :

h_a₀_fun :
  ∀ (f : ↥s₀),
    Function.Exact
      ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
          (HomologicalComplex.sc (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) fun f' ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) f' ⊓ C.left.basicOpen ↑f)

      ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
          (HomologicalComplex.sc (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) fun f' ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) f' ⊓ C.left.basicOpen ↑f)

Z₁ : (Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))
Z₂ : (Fin (n + 1) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))
Z₃ : (Fin (n + 2) → ↥s₀) → ModuleCat k :=
  fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i a))))

input.content:
# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-077 prover

## Outcome

- **File compiles cleanly** (0 errors).
- **Sorry count**: 7 → **6** (closed `f_R.map_smul'`).
- **No new axioms.**
- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (per user policy 2026-05-11).
- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** (per user policy). All checks via
  `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each edit.

## Sorries — old (iter-076) vs new (iter-077) mapping

| Line (iter-076) | Line (iter-077) | Declaration             | Status            |
|-----------------|-----------------|-------------------------|-------------------|
| L495            | L495            | substep (a) on `s`       | unchanged (dead end) |
| L819            | L819            | `h_π_split` (kernel-π)  | unchanged (dead end) |
| L847            | L847            | substep (a) on `s₀`     | unchanged (dead end) |
| L1077           | L1079           | `h_diff_pi_smul_f`      | unchanged (still sorry) |
| L1106           | —               | `f_R.map_smul'`         | **CLOSED** (-1) |
| L1116           | L1119           | `g_R.map_smul'`         | unchanged (still sorry, see g-side blocker) |
| L1145           | L1148           | `h_loc_exact`           | unchanged (still sorry) |

Net: **-1 sorry** (within budget; target was ≤ 5, achieved 6).

## What changed

### 1. Rebuilt `h_mod_X_i` to be a **literal** `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R`

`letI` (not `have`) so the module instance registers with typeclass synthesis,
and `exact e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` (not `convert h`) so the underlying smul
is exactly `e_i.toEquiv.smul R` — i.e. `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` by `rfl`
(`Equiv.smul_def` is itself `rfl`).

```lean
letI h_mod_X₁ : Module R scK₀.X₁ := by
  dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor,
    toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]
  letI := h_mod_pi₁
  exact e₁.toAddEquiv.module R
-- (analogous for X₂ and X₃; X₃ pre-rewrites `(up ℕ).next n` via `CochainComplex.next`)
```

The iter-076 analysis claim that `convert h` introduces "residual eta
congruence" turned out to be **partially correct in spirit but mis-diagnosed
in detail**: the actual blocker was the use of `have` (which does NOT register
as a typeclass instance), not the use of `convert`. With `letI` + `exact`,
the instance is both literal AND registered. The `Module R scK₀.X_i` instance
that typeclass synthesis finds for `r • x` is now literally
`e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` (visible in the goal state as
`h_mod_X₁ : Module ↑R ↑scK₀.X₁ := id (AddEquiv.module (↑R) e₁.toAddEquiv)`).

### 2. Closed `f_R.map_smul'`

Goal: `(scK₀.f)(r • x) = (RingHom.id R) r • (scK₀.f x)`.

```lean
intro r x
change (ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm (r • e₁ x)) =
  e₂.symm (r • e₂ ((ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) x))
apply e₂.injective
rw [LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply, h_diff_pi_smul_f r (e₁ x),
  LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
```

The `change` step relies on the literal `h_mod_X₁`/`h_mod_X₂` instances:
- `r • x = e₁.symm (r • e₁ x)` by `rfl` (literal `h_mod_X₁`).
- `r • (scK₀.f x) = e₂.symm (r • e₂ (scK₀.f x))` by `rfl` (literal `h_mod_X₂`).
- `(RingHom.id R) r = r` by `rfl`.

After the `change`, applying `e₂.injective` and rewriting via
`LinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply` (collapsing the outer `e₂ ∘ e₂.symm`),
`h_diff_pi_smul_f r (e₁ x)` (which fires because the resulting goal
matches its statement at `y := e₁ x`), and `LinearEquiv.symm_apply_apply`
(collapsing `e₁.symm (e₁ x)` to `x`) closes the goal.

### 3. Replaced `show` → `change` for the `CochainComplex.next` rewrite (linter)

`show K₀.X ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n) = K₀.X (n + 1)` changed the
goal, which triggered a `linter.style.show` warning. Replaced with
`change`, which is the correct tactic for explicit goal rewriting.

## What did NOT close

### `g_R.map_smul'` (L1119) — blocker is X₃ instance literality

The same `change` recipe used for `f_R.map_smul'` does not lift to the g-side:

| Step (f-side) | Lift to g-side?  | Reason                                            |
|---------------|------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| `r • x = e₂.symm (r • e₂ x)` | ✓ | `h_mod_X₂` is literal `e₂.toAddEquiv.module R` |
| `r • (scK₀.g x) = e₃.symm (r • e₃ (scK₀.g x))` | ✗ | `h_mod_X₃` is `Eq.mpr h_eq.symm (e₃.toAddEquiv.module R)` |

The issue: `↑scK₀.X₃ = ↑(K₀.X ((up ℕ).next n))` does NOT reduce to
`↑(K₀.X (n + 1))` (≅ `↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)`) by `rfl` — `(up ℕ).next n` is opaque
(`Classical.choose` over `Rel`).  The `h_mod_X₃` builder therefore uses
`rw [h_eq]` followed by `exact e₃.toAddEquiv.module R`, which makes
`h_mod_X₃ = Eq.mpr h_eq.symm (e₃.toAddEquiv.module R)`.  The
`r • z` for `z : scK₀.X₃` then carries an `Eq.mpr`-transport that
breaks the `change`-based literal alignment.

The verified attempt:
```lean
change (ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (r • e₂ x)) =
  r • (ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (e₂ x))
```
errors with `'change' tactic failed, pattern ... is not definitionally
equal to target ...` followed by `isDefEq` and `whnf` heartbeat
timeouts (cascade from the deep unfold attempt).

### Next iteration recipe for `g_R.map_smul'`

State a g-side analogue of `h_diff_pi_smul_f` with explicit `Eq.mpr`-casts
threaded through:

```lean
have h_diff_pi_smul_g : ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₂ i),
    letI := h_mod_pi₂
    letI := h_mod_pi₃
    e₃ (h_eq ▸ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (r • y)))) =
      r • e₃ (h_eq ▸ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm y))) := sorry
```
where `h_eq : scK₀.X₃ = K₀.X (n+1)`.  Then in `g_R.map_smul'`, `change`
the goal through `h_eq` so `r • (scK₀.g x)` becomes `Eq.mpr h_eq.symm
(r • (h_eq ▸ scK₀.g x))`, apply `e₃.injective`, and rewrite via
`h_diff_pi_smul_g`.  This converts the `g_R.map_smul'` sorry into a
`h_diff_pi_smul_g` sorry (no net sorry reduction unless that's then
proved).

### `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1079) — substantive 5-layer decomposition

The S1–S8 recipe (decompose alternating-sum cochain differential into
per-summand R-linearity, with `algebraMap_naturality` per restriction)
is documented in the existing comment block.  The iter-075/076 attempts
hit blockers at the `Pi.smul_apply / Finset.sum_apply / Finset.smul_sum`
fire step (no progress after the 5-layer unfold) and the
`Finset.sum_congr` shape-mismatch step.  No mechanical execution this
iteration — the recipe needs the cochain-factor identification chain
to be re-established before the inner manipulation can proceed.

### `h_loc_exact` (L1148) — product-localization commutation

Trying `LocalizedModule.map_exact (Submonoid.powers f.1) f_R g_R _`
would require `Function.Exact ⇑f_R ⇑g_R`, which (via `hf_eq`, `hg_eq`)
is exactly the OUTER goal `h_K₀_exact` we're trying to prove.  **Circular.**

The intended route remains: transport `h_a₀_fun f` (slice-cover exactness)
across the cochain-factor identification (product-localization commutation
at the finite cover `↑s₀`).  This is non-trivial Mathlib infrastructure
work — needs an explicit `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance on the
*product* `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ Z_i) ≅ ∏ᶜ LocalizedModule (powers f) Z_i`
for finite indexing.

## Diagnostics after repair

```
{"success": true, "items": [warnings only — no errors]}
```

Active warnings (all pre-existing, none new this iter):

- `declaration uses sorry` (L453) — the parent `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`.
- Three `simp [π]` flexible-tactic warnings at L796–803 (pre-existing).
- Seven `linter.style.longLine` warnings inside comment blocks at L986–1067
  (pre-existing).

## Blueprint markers

No blueprint editing this iteration (prover writes results, not blueprint).
The deterministic `sync_leanok` phase between prover and review will
adjust `\leanok` based on the new sorry count.  Blueprint chapter:
`blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex § Čech acyclicity`.

The blueprint chapter file `blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cohomology_BasicOpenCech.tex`
listed in the dispatch prompt does NOT exist; the actual content lives
in `Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` (which is already in place from iter-076).
No new blueprint file is needed.

## Repair budget

- Starts: 7 sorries.
- Plan target: ≤ 5 (close 2+).
- Hard cap: 7 (no regression).
- Ends: **6 sorries** (-1 net; close of `f_R.map_smul'`).

The target of -2 was not met because the g-side requires non-trivial
`Eq.mpr`-cast handling that would re-introduce a sorry rather than
eliminate one.

## Key insights for the plan agent

1. **`letI` + `exact`** is the right idiom for installing a transported
   module instance — NOT `have` + `convert`.  The iter-076 diagnosis
   ("residual eta-congruence from `convert`") was partially correct but
   missed that `have` doesn't register as instance for typeclass
   synthesis.  With `letI` (registers as instance) + `exact` (no
   congruence peeling), the instance is both literal and discoverable.

2. **`change` + `e_i.injective` transport** works for `f_R.map_smul'`
   because both `h_mod_X₁` and `h_mod_X₂` are literal.  It does NOT work
   for `g_R.map_smul'` because `h_mod_X₃` has a non-literal `Eq.mpr`
   transport (forced by `ComplexShape.next` opacity).

3. **`h_loc_exact` cannot be derived from `LocalizedModule.map_exact`**
   applied to `Function.Exact ⇑f_R ⇑g_R` — that's circular with the
   outer goal `h_K₀_exact`.  The intended product-localization
   commutation route requires explicit `IsLocalizedModule.Away`
   instances on the cochain-product, which is non-trivial Mathlib
   infrastructure.

## Next-iteration prover handoff

In dependency order (lowest-hanging first):

1. **`g_R.map_smul'` (L1119)** — state a *new* `h_diff_pi_smul_g` with
   explicit `Eq.mpr h_eq.symm`-casts on the codomain so the statement
   type-checks; then mirror the iter-077 `f_R.map_smul'` recipe with
   `change`-through-`h_eq`.  Net: trade L1119 for L`new h_diff_pi_smul_g`.

2. **`h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1079)** — substantive content; needs the
   5-layer functor stack to be unfolded carefully (S1–S8 recipe).
   Documented in-place at L1009–1067.

3. **`h_loc_exact` (L1148)** — needs an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
   instance on `∏ᶜ Z_i` (or equivalently on `scK₀.X_i = K₀.X i`).
   This is the finite product-localization commutation theorem; not
   yet in Mathlib for the general curve setting but tractable for the
   finite `↑s₀` indexing.

4. **L495, L819, L847** — confirmed dead-ends per multiple iterations;
   stay sorry'd unless user supplies new hypothesis.
